sections.paragraph: an unused root. Arab. حَمُتَ to be hot, warm (spoken of the day), to become stagnant, or rancid (as water, butter, etc.); whence حَمِيتُ and تَحْمُوتُ a bottle and its contents become rancid; unless that root should rather be secondary and derived from these nouns; the primary root being حَمِطَ to be hot; whence حميت for حمية. Either from חָמַת or from חָמָה is-
